# Financial Deep Research: AngloGold Ashanti PLC (AU)

Research conducted: 2026-05-11
Stage 1 baseline provided: No — §9 will note the absence.

## 1. Recent Financial Performance & Quarterly Trends

### Most Recent Report
AngloGold Ashanti reported Q1 2026 earnings on 8 May 2026. The quarter delivered record financials even as production was broadly flat year-over-year. Gold production for the Group rose 1% year-on-year to 724,000 oz (720,000 oz in Q1 2025), with managed operations up 1% to 666,000 oz while non-managed joint ventures fell 8% to 58,000 oz. The average realized gold price was $4,863/oz, up 69% from $2,874/oz in Q1 2025. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

Financial highlights for Q1 2026 included:
- Revenue of approximately $3.15 billion (per consensus data; the company reported headline earnings but did not prominently disclose top-line revenue in the preliminary release). [Source: Quiver Quantitative / Title: ANGLOGOLD ASHANTI ($AU) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings / Date: 2026-05-08]
- Adjusted EBITDA of $2.3 billion, up 130% year-on-year.
- Free cash flow of $1.2 billion, up 190% year-on-year and a new quarterly record.
- Headline earnings of $1.3 billion, up 187% year-on-year.
- Basic earnings of $1.28 billion, up 189% year-on-year.
- Net cash flow from operating activities of $1.7 billion, up 136%. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

Costs rose in line with gold-price-linked royalties and industry inflation. Group total cash costs increased 14% year-on-year to $1,391/oz, and AISC rose 19% to $1,955/oz. Management emphasized that *underlying* operational costs actually fell $22/oz, with the headline increase driven by a $117/oz rise in royalties (mechanically linked to the record gold price), $43/oz in inflationary pressures (labour and mining contractors), and $30/oz in FX movements. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

Capital expenditure in Q1 2026 was $467 million, up 39% year-on-year, split between $305 million in sustaining capex and $162 million in non-sustaining capex directed largely at Nevada growth projects. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

The balance sheet strengthened further, with net cash of $868 million at 31 March 2026 versus net debt of $755 million a year earlier. On 16 April 2026, the company bought back approximately $666 million principal amount of outstanding bonds to optimize the capital structure. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

Most recent as of: 2026-05-08

### Most Recent Full Year Report
For the year ended 31 December 2025, AngloGold Ashanti reported record operational and financial performance. Gold production increased 16% year-on-year to 3.091 million oz (from 2.661 million oz in 2024), driven primarily by the first full-year contribution from Sukari (acquired via Centamin in November 2024) and improved performance at Obuasi, Siguiri, Geita, and Cerro Vanguardia. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

The average realized gold price for 2025 was $3,468/oz, up 45% from $2,394/oz in 2024. Key full-year financial metrics:
- Adjusted EBITDA: $6.3 billion (+129% year-on-year).
- Free cash flow: $2.9 billion (+204% year-on-year).
- Headline earnings: $2.7 billion (+186% year-on-year).
- Basic earnings: $2.6 billion (+163% year-on-year).
- Net cash flow from operating activities: $4.784 billion (+143% year-on-year). [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

The company declared total dividends of $1.8 billion for 2025 (357 US cents per share), representing more than 50% of free cash flow. Adjusted net cash at year-end was $879 million, a swing from adjusted net debt of $567 million at the end of 2024. Total liquidity stood at approximately $4.4 billion, including cash and equivalents of roughly $2.9 billion and undrawn credit facilities. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] *** [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

Total cash costs for the Group were $1,242/oz (up 7% year-on-year) and AISC was $1,709/oz (up 6% year-on-year). For managed operations, TCC was $1,252/oz and AISC was $1,751/oz. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

Capital expenditure for 2025 totaled approximately $1.6 billion. The company also paid $1.137 billion in cash taxes and $380 million in royalties to host governments during the year. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

Most recent full year as of: 2026-02-20 (Q4 2025 release); 2026-03-16 (Annual Report)

### Key Business Drivers in Recent Quarters
The dominant financial driver across all recent periods is the realized gold price. In 2025, the 45% increase in the average gold price received translated into a 143% rise in operating cash flow, demonstrating extreme revenue leverage. Volume growth (notably Sukari) amplified this effect. In Q1 2026, the realized price surged further to $4,863/oz, up 69% year-on-year, driving another step-change in margins despite cost inflation. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

Cost performance has been a secondary but important driver. While nominal TCC and AISC have risen, the company emphasizes that underlying operational costs (excluding price-linked royalties and FX) have been contained. The Full Asset Potential (FAP) programme has delivered productivity improvements at Sukari (TCG $783/oz in its first full year), Geita, and Cuiabá. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

### Segment Performance
The portfolio is organized geographically: Africa (DRC, Egypt, Ghana, Guinea, Tanzania), Australia, and the Americas (Argentina, Brazil, and US projects).

**Africa** is the largest contributor. For the six months ended 30 June 2025, Africa generated $3.19 billion in gold income versus $1.70 billion in the prior-year period. Key assets include:
- **Sukari (Egypt):** Produced 500,000 oz in its first full year under AngloGold Ashanti ownership (2025) at a TCC of $783/oz. It contributed 460,000 oz of the year-on-year production increase.
- **Geita (Tanzania):** Solid performer; Q1 2026 production up 12,000 oz year-on-year.
- **Obuasi (Ghana):** Production increased 45,000 oz year-on-year in 2025 and continued to ramp in Q1 2026 (+8,000 oz), though the asset has experienced safety incidents including a contractor fatality in April 2026.
- **Siguiri (Guinea):** Up 16,000 oz year-on-year in 2025.
- **Kibali (DRC, 45% attributable):** Lower production in Q1 2026, contributing to the non-managed JV decline.
- **Iduapriem (Ghana):** Steady contributions. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] *** [Source: Company Q2 2025 Earnings Release / Title: AGA Q2 Jun 2025 Earnings Release / Date: 2025-08-01] ***

**Australia** contributed $797 million in gold income in H1 2025 versus $561 million in H1 2024. Assets include Sunrise Dam and Tropicana (70% attributable). Q1 2026 saw Sunrise Dam production decline by 10,000 oz year-on-year due to mine sequencing. [Source: Company Q2 2025 Earnings Release / Title: AGA Q2 Jun 2025 Earnings Release / Date: 2025-08-01] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

**Americas** contributed $764 million in gold income in H1 2025 versus $571 million in H1 2024. Cerro Vanguardia (Argentina) and Cuiabá (Brazil) are the main operations. Serra Grande was sold on 1 December 2025. Cuiabá saw a 9,000 oz production increase in Q1 2026. The Americas segment also includes the Nevada growth projects (Arthur Gold and North Bullfrog). [Source: Company Q2 2025 Earnings Release / Title: AGA Q2 Jun 2025 Earnings Release / Date: 2025-08-01] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

### Geographic & FX Exposure
Approximately two-thirds of production value is generated in Africa, with significant exposure to the Tanzanian shilling, Ghanaian cedi, Guinean franc, and Egyptian pound. The company notes that FX movements provided a cumulative 2% cost benefit in 2025 as several operating currencies depreciated against the US dollar. In Q1 2026, however, FX movements added an estimated $30/oz to costs, suggesting some reversal of prior tailwinds. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

Revenue is entirely US dollar-denominated (gold is priced in USD), providing a natural hedge against local currency operating costs. The main FX risk to earnings is the translation of local operating costs and capex into USD; however, the company's margin structure is sufficiently wide at current gold prices that moderate FX volatility is not a material threat to profitability.

## 2. Growth Trajectory

### Trajectory Assessment
The default 2-year lookback window is appropriate but must be contextualized by the transformative Centamin acquisition (November 2024) and the exceptional gold price environment.

- **2023:** Revenue of $4.6 billion; Adjusted EBITDA of $1.42 billion; free cash flow of $109 million; attributable loss of ($235 million). Production was approximately 2.5 million oz. The year was burdened by corporate restructuring costs ($314 million) and impairments in Brazil. [Source: Company Annual Report 2023 / Title: 2023 Annual Report Snapshot / Date: 2025-01-29] ***
- **2024:** Revenue of $5.8 billion (revenue from product sales); Adjusted EBITDA of $2.75 billion; free cash flow of $942 million; basic earnings of $1.0 billion. Production of 2.661 million oz. This year included the Centamin acquisition and initial Sukari contributions. [Source: Company 2024 Annual Report Financial Review / Title: Financial Review 2024 / Date: 2025-03-26] *** [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***
- **2025:** Adjusted EBITDA of $6.3 billion; free cash flow of $2.9 billion; basic earnings of $2.6 billion. Production of 3.091 million oz. The step-up was driven by Sukari's first full year, higher gold prices, and operational improvements. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

The trajectory is sharply upward over the past two years, but the 2025-2026 growth is heavily dependent on gold price rather than volume. Production guidance for 2026 is 2.80–3.17 million oz, which brackets the 2025 outcome and implies flattish volume growth. The underlying volume story is therefore one of portfolio transformation (Sukari in, Serra Grande out) rather than organic expansion at this stage.

Future volume growth hinges on:
1. **Nevada greenfield projects:** Arthur Gold Project (4.9 Moz reserve, ~500,000 oz/year at $954/oz AISC, $3.6 billion project capital, FID expected H2 2026) and North Bullfrog (~105,000 oz/year first five years, $480 million capital, FID H2 2026, construction 2027). [Source: Company Technical Report Summary / Title: Arthur Gold Project Technical Report Summary / Date: 2026-03-26] ***
2. **Brownfield expansions:** Sukari, Geita, and Cuiabá brownfield opportunities are expected to be detailed during 2026. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***
3. **Reserve growth:** The company added more than 9 million oz of new Mineral Reserve in 2025, roughly three times depletion, marking the ninth consecutive year of reserve growth before depletion. Total Group gold Mineral Reserve at 31 December 2025 was 36.5 million oz (+17% from 31.2 million oz). [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

### Peer-Relative Growth Context
AngloGold Ashanti produced 3.09 million oz in 2025, ranking it among the top five global gold miners. Newmont remains the largest at ~5.9 million oz, followed by Agnico Eagle (~3.45 million oz), Barrick (~3.26 million oz), and Navoi (~3.15 million oz). AngloGold's 16% production growth in 2025 outperformed most peers, but this was largely acquisition-driven (Sukari) rather than organic. Newmont's production declined year-on-year, while Barrick's fell 17% on divestitures. [Source: Mining Magazine / Title: The largest gold miners of 2025 / Date: 2026-04-08] *

The company's reserve replacement ratio (3x depletion) appears industry-leading, though peer comparisons on this metric vary by reporting methodology.

## 3. Margin & Profitability Profile

For a gold miner, the most meaningful margin metrics are the margin per ounce relative to the realized gold price—specifically total cash cost (TCC) margin and all-in sustaining cost (AISC) margin. These capture operating leverage and the full cost of maintaining production, respectively.

**2025 Margins:**
- Realized gold price: $3,468/oz
- Group TCC: $1,242/oz → TCC margin: $2,226/oz (64%)
- Group AISC: $1,709/oz → AISC margin: $1,759/oz (51%)
- Managed operations TCC: $1,252/oz; AISC: $1,751/oz [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

**Q1 2026 Margins:**
- Realized gold price: $4,863/oz
- Group TCC: $1,391/oz → TCC margin: $3,472/oz (71%)
- Group AISC: $1,955/oz → AISC margin: $2,908/oz (60%) [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

These margins are exceptionally wide by historical industry standards and are the direct source of the record free cash flow. The key question for forward profitability is whether the company can maintain cost discipline as the gold price rises. Management's "Full Asset Potential" programme and emphasis on underlying cost control (claiming a $22/oz reduction in Q1 2026 before royalties, inflation, and FX) suggest operational discipline, but the mechanical link between royalties and gold price means that nominal costs will rise with the gold price even if operational efficiency is stable. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

IFRS profitability also improved dramatically: basic earnings of $2.6 billion in 2025 versus $1.0 billion in 2024, with the effective tax rate influenced by jurisdictional mix and a $6 million Pillar Two top-up tax provision recognized in 2024. [Source: Company 2024 Form 20-F / Title: Form 20-F for year ended 31 December 2024 / Date: 2025-04-15] ***

### Peer-Relative Profitability Context
Newmont guided to 2025 AISC of approximately $1,400/oz, while the top ten global gold miners averaged AISC of $1,571/oz in 2025. AngloGold's Group AISC of $1,709/oz sits above this peer average, placing it in the second quartile on costs rather than the first. However, the acquisition of Sukari (TCC $783/oz) and the Nevada projects ($954/oz AISC targeted) should improve the portfolio mix over time. [Source: Mining Magazine / Title: The largest gold miners of 2025 / Date: 2026-04-08] * [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] *** [Source: Matrix BCG / Title: What is Competitive Landscape of Newmont Mining Company? / Date: 2026-03-29] *

## 4. Cash Generation & Quality

### Cash Generation Quality Assessment
Free cash flow is the primary cash metric for AngloGold Ashanti, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditure (managed operations). In 2025, the company generated $4.784 billion in net cash from operating activities and $2.9 billion in free cash flow—triple the 2024 level. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

The quality of this cash generation is high but entirely dependent on the gold price. The company is fully unhedged, meaning cash flows represent pure spot price exposure. This is a deliberate strategic choice that maximizes upside at the cost of downside volatility. Given current gold prices, operating cash conversion is robust; however, if gold prices were to revert to 2023 levels (~$1,950/oz spot), the same cost structure would generate materially lower free cash flow.

A key quality indicator is the ratio of free cash flow to adjusted EBITDA. In 2025, FCF of $2.9 billion represented approximately 46% of adjusted EBITDA ($6.3 billion). This is a healthy conversion ratio for a mining company with meaningful sustaining and growth capex demands. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

### Working Capital & Cash Conversion
Working capital movements in 2025 were a modest drag. The annual report discloses:
- Increase in inventories: ($57 million)
- Increase in trade and other receivables: ($219 million)
- Increase in trade and other payables: +$102 million
- Net working capital outflow: approximately ($174 million) [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

This is not material relative to $4.8 billion of operating cash flow. For a gold miner, working capital dynamics are typically less consequential than for manufacturing or retail businesses. Inventory consists largely of ore stockpiles and gold-in-circuit, while receivables are usually short-dated (gold sales settle quickly). The 2025 increase in receivables likely reflects higher gold prices and timing of sales at year-end.

Capital expenditure in 2025 totaled approximately $1.6 billion. The split between sustaining and non-sustaining is not precisely disclosed in the sources obtained, but 2026 guidance implies a heavy non-sustaining load: total capex guidance of $1.04–1.14 billion includes non-sustaining capex of $960 million–$1.06 billion, suggesting sustaining capex of only ~$80–180 million. This appears low and may reflect a reclassification or timing effect; historically, sustaining capex for the Group has run closer to $600–800 million annually. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] *** [Source: Morningstar / Title: ANG - Anglogold Ashanti PLC Earnings Call Transcripts / Date: 2026-02-18] **

### Earnings Quality
Headline earnings ($2.7 billion in 2025) and basic earnings ($2.6 billion) are closely aligned, suggesting limited distortion from non-recurring items. The main adjustments in 2025 were:
- A $98 million impairment of the Quebradona exploration project in Colombia due to regulatory/political uncertainty.
- A $47 million loss on disposal of the Doropo and ABC projects.
- Organizational redesign and restructuring costs.
- Integration costs related to Centamin. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

These one-off costs totaled roughly $200+ million pre-tax but were manageable relative to the earnings base. Earnings quality appears high: the profit stream is cash-backed, with operating cash flow of $4.8 billion well exceeding reported net income.

## 5. Balance Sheet & Liquidity

Total borrowings at 31 December 2025 were $2.044 billion (2024: $1.984 billion). The debt portfolio consists principally of three rated bonds issued by AngloGold Ashanti Holdings plc and guaranteed by AngloGold Ashanti plc:
- **$750 million** aggregate principal of 3.375% notes due 1 November 2028.
- **$700 million** aggregate principal of 3.75% notes due 1 October 2030.
- **$300 million** aggregate principal of 6.50% notes due 15 April 2040. [Source: Company Form 20-F 2025 / Title: Form 20-F for year ended 31 December 2025 / Date: 2026-03-26] ***

In April 2026, the company launched capped cash tender offers for up to $650 million of these notes. By the early tender deadline (13 April 2026), investors had tendered approximately 74% of the 2028 notes, 64% of the 2030 notes, and 26% of the 2040 notes. The company ultimately bought back approximately $666 million principal amount on 16 April 2026. [Source: Company Press Release via BusinessWire / Title: AngloGold Ashanti Holdings plc Announces Capped Cash Tender Offers / Date: 2026-03-30] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

In addition to the bonds, the company maintains a $1.4 billion multi-currency revolving credit facility (maturity June 2029), a $65 million Siguiri revolving credit facility, and a $5 million Geita facility. All were undrawn as of 31 December 2025. [Source: Company Q4 2025 Earnings Release / Title: AGA Q4 Dec 2025 Earnings Release / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

Liquidity at year-end 2025 was approximately $4.4 billion, comprising $2.9 billion in cash and equivalents and undrawn facilities. Adjusted net cash was $879 million, compared to adjusted net debt of $567 million at the end of 2024. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

Credit ratings as of early 2026:
- **Moody's:** Baa3, Stable.
- **S&P Global:** BB+, Positive (outlook revised to positive from stable in November 2025).
- **Fitch:** BBB-, Stable (outlook revised to stable from negative in December 2024). [Source: Company Debt Capital Markets Page / Title: Debt Capital Markets / Date: 2026-05-05] *** [Source: S&P Global / Title: Research Update: AngloGold Ashanti PLC Outlook Revised To Positive / Date: 2025-11-05] ** [Source: Fitch Ratings / Title: Fitch Revises AngloGold Ashanti's Outlook to Stable / Date: 2024-12-10] **

### Debt Maturity Profile & Refinancing Risk
The three bond tranches mature in 2028, 2030, and 2040, with no material maturities before November 2028. The April 2026 tender offer reduced the 2028 and 2030 tranches meaningfully, pushing out refinancing risk. The RCF matures in June 2029. Given the net cash position, $4.4 billion in liquidity, and strong gold price cash flows, refinancing risk appears negligible. The company has no financial covenant pressure; the leverage ratio (adjusted net debt to adjusted EBITDA) was negative (net cash) at year-end 2025. [Source: Company Q2 2025 Earnings Results / Title: AGA Q2 Jun 2025 Earnings Results / Date: 2025-08-01] *** [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***

### Assessment
The balance sheet is in its strongest position in company history, as repeatedly emphasized by management. The "fortress balance sheet" characterization is supported by the net cash position, long-dated maturities, undrawn credit lines, and strong coverage ratios. The main vulnerability is not refinancing risk but rather the potential for rapid leverage accumulation if gold prices fall sharply and the company maintains aggressive capital returns and growth capex simultaneously.

## 6. Capital Deployment & Returns on Capital

### Share Repurchases
Historically, AngloGold Ashanti has not been a significant buyer of its own stock. However, on 7 May 2026, the Board approved a proposed share repurchase programme of up to $2.0 billion, subject to shareholder approval. This represents a material shift in capital allocation and is explicitly intended to "align the Company's capital return framework with its North American peers." [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

### Dividends
The company overhauled its dividend policy in 2024-2025. The current framework comprises:
- A base dividend of $0.50 per share annually ($0.125 per quarter).
- A variable top-up dividend to achieve a 50% payout of free cash flow.
- The policy is subject to maintaining adjusted net debt / adjusted EBITDA below 1.0x. [Source: Company 2024 Annual Report Strategic Report / Title: 2024 AGA UK Annual Report Strategic Report / Date: 2025-03-26] ***

In 2025, total dividends declared were $1.8 billion (357 US cents per share), equivalent to roughly 62% of free cash flow. The Q4 2025 interim dividend alone was $875 million (173 cents per share), and the Q1 2026 interim dividend was $585 million (116 cents per share). Cumulative dividends from 2021 to 2025 totaled $2.7 billion. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

### M&A / Capex
**M&A:** The Centamin acquisition (November 2024, ~$2.5 billion) added Sukari and was the transformative deal of the cycle. In 2025, the company acquired Augusta Gold in Nevada for $158 million to consolidate landholdings around the Beatty district. It also divested non-core assets: the Doropo and ABC projects (sold to Resolute Mining for $162 million total consideration, resulting in a $47 million loss) and Serra Grande in Brazil (sold 1 December 2025 for $52 million net proceeds). [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

**Capex:** 2025 capital expenditure was approximately $1.6 billion. Growth (non-sustaining) capex is directed at Nevada projects, waste stripping at Sukari, and brownfield expansions. 2026 guidance calls for total capex of $1.04–1.14 billion, with non-sustaining capex of $960 million–$1.06 billion. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] *** [Source: Morningstar / Title: ANG - Anglogold Ashanti PLC Earnings Call Transcripts / Date: 2026-02-18] **

### Returns on Capital
The company does not prominently disclose Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) or Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) in the filings obtained. However, the economics of the current portfolio can be inferred from project-level disclosures:
- Sukari: TCC of $783/oz in 2025 implies a project-level margin of ~$2,700/oz at 2025 prices, suggesting extremely high returns on the Centamin purchase price.
- Arthur Gold Project (Nevada): PFS indicates AISC of $954/oz over an initial nine-year life. At a $4,000+/oz gold price environment, this implies robust unlevered returns, though the $3.6 billion project capital requirement is substantial relative to the company's current enterprise value. [Source: Company Technical Report Summary / Title: Arthur Gold Project Technical Report Summary / Date: 2026-03-26] ***

At the consolidated level, basic earnings of $2.6 billion against shareholders' equity of roughly $9–10 billion implies a return on equity in the mid-to-high 20% range—exceptional for a mining company and well above any reasonable estimate of the cost of capital.

## 7. Management Guidance & Forward Outlook

### Most Recent Full Year Report Guidance (2026)
On the Q4 2025 earnings call and in subsequent filings, management provided the following 2026 guidance, which was reaffirmed in the Q1 2026 release:
- **Gold production:** 2.80–3.17 million oz.
- **Group AISC:** $1,780–$1,990/oz.
- **Total capital expenditure:** $1.04–1.14 billion. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

### Most Recent Report Guidance
Q1 2026 production of 724,000 oz keeps the company on track to meet full-year guidance. Management noted that "the Company remains on track to meet its 2026 annual guidance." [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

### Management Commentary
CEO Alberto Calderon has emphasized a focus on "controlling what we can control"—managing underlying costs and ensuring safe, predictable operating results. Strategic priorities highlighted repeatedly include:
- Delivery of predictable operating results.
- Competitive shareholder returns (dividends + the new buyback authorization).
- Bringing a new production center into operation in southern Nevada.
- Steady ramp-up of Obuasi.
- Organic growth projects in Tanzania, Guinea, Egypt, and Brazil. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

The Board's decision to declare a $2 billion share repurchase programme and to pay out more than 50% of free cash flow as dividends signals high confidence in the forward cash generation capacity of the business.

### Forward Claim Confidence Weighting
- **2026 production guidance (2.80–3.17 Moz): Medium-High confidence.** The range is relatively wide. Sukari's performance is proven, but Obuasi's ramp-up has historically been volatile, and mine sequencing variations (e.g., Sunrise Dam, Kibali) create quarterly volatility.
- **2026 AISC guidance ($1,780–$1,990/oz): Medium confidence.** The lower bound assumes no further inflationary pressure and stable FX. The upper bound allows for royalty escalation and cost inflation. Given Q1 2026 AISC was $1,955/oz, the company is already near the top of the range; achieving the midpoint likely requires operational improvements through the year.
- **Nevada project timelines (FID H2 2026, construction 2027): Medium confidence.** Permitting activities are "well underway" for North Bullfrog, and the Arthur PFS is complete. However, greenfield projects in the US face permitting and environmental review risks that could delay FID.
- **Dividend sustainability at 50%+ of FCF: High confidence in the policy, Medium confidence in the absolute dollar amount.** The policy is explicitly tied to the leverage ceiling (1.0x net debt/EBITDA). As long as gold prices remain above $3,000/oz and production stays near 3 Moz, the payout is affordable. A sustained drop below $2,500/oz would likely force a reduction.

## 8. Material Financial Risks & One-Off Items

**Colombia Asset Stranding:** The company fully impaired exploration and evaluation costs of $98 million relating to the Quebradona project in 2025 due to a heightened political climate and uncertainty regarding mining and environmental policy. Colombian government actions—Resolution 865 of 2025 declaring a protected area overlapping the project, Resolution 384 of 2025 declaring a food production protected area, and the February 2026 notification of a sanctioning process related to alleged unauthorized pruning near La Colosa—suggest ongoing regulatory risk. While these are non-core assets, further write-downs or legal costs are possible. [Source: Company Form 20-F 2025 / Title: Form 20-F for year ended 31 December 2025 / Date: 2026-03-26] ***

**Obuasi Safety and Operational Disruption:** Obuasi has experienced significant challenges, including security-related fatalities in 2025 stemming from clashes between public security forces and illegal miners. A contractor fatality occurred on 24 April 2026 (post-Q1 end) due to a waste material release from an underground ore pass. These incidents create operational, reputational, and potentially financial risks (regulatory scrutiny, production interruptions). [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] *** [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

**Cost Inflation and Royalty Escalation:** While underlying operational costs are being managed, the mechanical linkage of royalties to gold price means that a $100/oz move in gold attracts approximately $5/oz in royalties. At $4,500+/oz gold, this creates a structural cost headwind that offsets some of the revenue benefit. Additionally, labour and contractor costs are rising across the portfolio. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***

**DRC Fiscal Uncertainty:** The 2025 DRC Finance Act removed custom duty exemptions on gold exports (imposing a 3% duty) and introduced a priority dividend right for the DRC government. The company also has an attributable $65 million in outstanding VAT receivables from the DRC government with uncertain timing of recovery. [Source: Company Form 20-F 2024 / Title: Form 20-F for year ended 31 December 2024 / Date: 2025-04-15] ***

**Tax and BEPS Pillar Two:** The UK implementation of Pillar Two global minimum tax (15%) exposes the company to top-up tax liabilities if effective tax rates in operating jurisdictions fall below 15%. A $6 million estimated current tax expense was recognized for 2024. This is currently immaterial but could grow. [Source: Company Form 20-F 2024 / Title: Form 20-F for year ended 31 December 2024 / Date: 2025-04-15] ***

**Foreign Exchange:** Local currency depreciations provided a 2% cost benefit in 2025, but Q1 2026 saw a $30/oz FX cost impact. Further USD weakness or local currency strength would pressure margins. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

## 9. Discrepancies vs. Structured Data Baseline

### Research Data vs. Stage 1 Baseline
Stage 1 baseline not provided at runtime — section deferred.

## 10. Key Financial Assumptions Embedded in Current Valuation

As of early May 2026, AngloGold Ashanti trades at approximately $102 per share, implying a market capitalization of roughly $50–55 billion and a trailing P/E in the 15–18x range. The current valuation appears to embed the following key financial assumptions:

1. **Sustained high gold prices:** The P/E of ~15–18x on 2025 earnings implies the market does not expect the current ~$4,500–$5,000/oz gold price environment to persist indefinitely; otherwise, the multiple would likely be higher given the earnings momentum. Conversely, a normalization to $2,500/oz would render this P/E misleadingly low on a forward basis. The valuation likely assumes a long-term gold price comfortably above $3,000/oz.

2. **Production stability at 3.0–3.2 Moz/year:** The market is pricing in successful execution of the 2026 guidance and assumes no major operational disappointments at Sukari, Obuasi, or Geita.

3. **AISC containment below $2,000/oz:** With Q1 2026 AISC already at $1,955/oz, the market appears to accept that costs will run near the upper end of guidance but will not spiral out of control.

4. **Nevada projects deliver on economics:** The Arthur and North Bullfrog projects are being valued as high-margin, long-life assets in a Tier-1 jurisdiction. Delays or capex overruns would likely compress the valuation.

5. **Capital return framework is durable:** The $2 billion buyback authorization and 50% FCF dividend payout are being priced as sustainable. Any forced retrenchment of returns would likely be penalized.

6. **Reserve replacement continues:** The market appears to give the company credit for its 9-year track record of reserve growth and the 36.5 Moz reserve base, assuming continued replenishment at conservative gold price assumptions.

## 11. Bottom-Line Financial Assessment

### Business Model Characterization
AngloGold Ashanti is a senior gold producer with a globally diversified portfolio weighted toward Africa. The business model is characterized by:
- **High operating leverage to gold price:** Unhedged production means revenue, EBITDA, and free cash flow are highly sensitive to the realized gold price.
- **Acquisition-driven growth with organic optionality:** The Centamin deal transformed the production base. Future growth depends on a mix of high-quality brownfield expansions (lower risk, lower capex) and greenfield development in Nevada (higher risk, higher capex, but premier jurisdiction).
- **Capital-intensive with long-dated assets:** Mining requires sustained sustaining capex and significant non-sustaining capex for reserve replacement. The company has historically under-invested relative to peers but is now ramping Nevada capex.
- **Jurisdictional risk concentration:** A majority of production and cash flow is generated in Africa, exposing the company to political, fiscal, and security risks that are absent from the peer average.

The most meaningful financial lenses for this business are: (1) margin per ounce (TCC and AISC vs. realized price), (2) free cash flow conversion, (3) reserve replacement ratio, and (4) balance sheet flexibility (net debt/EBITDA). Traditional manufacturing metrics like gross margin are less informative because cost of sales includes non-cash amortization and because the value chain is radically different.

### Strengths (confidence weighted)
- **Balance sheet strength and liquidity: High confidence.** The net cash position ($879 million at year-end 2025; $868 million at Q1 2026), $4.4 billion in liquidity, and long-dated debt maturities provide exceptional financial flexibility. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***
- **Cash flow generation at current gold prices: High confidence.** The unhedged, low-cost (relative to current price) portfolio is generating record free cash flow. Q1 2026 FCF of $1.2 billion annualizes to ~$4.8 billion, suggesting the current run-rate is extraordinarily high. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***
- **Reserve growth and replacement: High confidence.** Nine consecutive years of reserve growth before depletion, with 9 Moz added in 2025 alone (3x depletion). The 36.5 Moz reserve base supports a 12+ year mine life at current production rates. [Source: Company Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results / Title: Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results Presentation / Date: 2026-02-20] ***
- **Sukari acquisition integration: High confidence.** First-year production of 500,000 oz at $783/oz TCC confirms the asset is a cash-generative cornerstone. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***
- **Cost discipline (underlying): Medium-High confidence.** The FAP programme has delivered underlying cost reductions that partly offset inflation. Management's focus on "controlling what we can control" is backed by data showing $22/oz underlying reductions in Q1 2026. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

### Weaknesses (confidence weighted)
- **Jurisdictional and political risk exposure: High confidence.** Operations in the DRC, Guinea, Ghana, Tanzania, Argentina, and Brazil carry material political, fiscal, and security risks. The Colombia write-off and DRC VAT/fiscal changes are concrete examples. [Source: Company Form 20-F 2025 / Title: Form 20-F for year ended 31 December 2025 / Date: 2026-03-26] *** [Source: Company Form 20-F 2024 / Title: Form 20-F for year ended 31 December 2024 / Date: 2025-04-15] ***
- **Safety record and operational hazards at Obuasi: High confidence.** Fatalities in 2025 and 2026 highlight persistent safety challenges, particularly related to illegal mining incursions. This creates regulatory and production risks. [Source: Company Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Title: Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration / Date: 2026-05-08] *** [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***
- **Cost inflation and royalty linkage: High confidence.** While underlying costs are controlled, the mechanical link between royalties and gold price means that nominal AISC will rise as gold rises. The company is not a pure margin-expansion story at higher gold prices. [Source: Company 2025 UK Annual Report / Title: 2025 AGA UK Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-16] ***
- **Relative cost position vs. peers: Medium-High confidence.** Group AISC of $1,709/oz (2025) is above the top-ten peer average of ~$1,571/oz. The company is not the lowest-cost producer among seniors. [Source: Mining Magazine / Title: The largest gold miners of 2025 / Date: 2026-04-08] *
- **Dependence on gold price for valuation: High confidence.** The current financial profile is entirely a function of the gold price. A reversion to 2023 price levels ($1,900–$2,000/oz spot) would collapse free cash flow and likely force dividend cuts and capex deferrals.

### Watch Items
- **Nevada project execution:** FID timing, permitting progress, and capex control for Arthur and North Bullfrog are critical to the 2027–2030 growth narrative.
- **Obuasi stability:** Whether the safety incidents and illegal mining incursions can be contained without production disruptions or regulatory intervention.
- **Q1 2026 AISC trajectory:** AISC of $1,955/oz is already at the upper end of 2026 guidance ($1,780–$1,990/oz). If costs do not improve through the year, guidance may be breached or revised.
- **Gold price sensitivity:** The company is fully unhedged. Any correction in gold would immediately flow through to revenue and cash flow.
- **Share buyback execution:** The proposed $2 billion programme is a new tool for the company. The pace and price at which it is executed will affect per-share returns and balance sheet flexibility.
- **Credit rating actions:** S&P's positive outlook suggests a potential upgrade. A move to investment grade (BBB-) would be a positive catalyst for the cost of capital.

## 12. Data Gaps & Limitations

1. **Exact IFRS revenue for 2025:** No data found for the precise IFRS revenue figure for the full year 2025; search scope included the Q4 2025 earnings presentation, 2025 UK annual report, and 20-F filing. Gold income segment data from H1 2025 allows an approximation of ~$10.7 billion, but the exact reported revenue number was not retrieved. Analytical impact: limits precise margin and turnover ratio calculations.

2. **Exact 2025 sustaining vs. non-sustaining capex split:** While total 2025 capex of ~$1.6 billion is cited, the precise split between sustaining and non-sustaining was not found in the sources obtained. Analytical impact: limits assessment of maintenance capital intensity and true "maintenance" free cash flow.

3. **Detailed debt maturity schedule beyond the three rated bonds:** The 20-F confirms $2.044 billion in total borrowings but the exact maturity profile of any bank debt or leases beyond the $750M/$700M/$300M bonds was not fully detailed. Analytical impact: minor, given the net cash position and long-dated bond maturities.

4. **Q1 2026 segment-level production and cost breakdown:** The Q1 2026 release provides group-level costs and selected mine-level production changes, but a full segmental P&L (Africa, Australia, Americas) was not included in the preliminary release obtained. Analytical impact: limits granular peer-style segment comparison for the most recent quarter.

5. **Historical data staleness for peer comparisons:** The peer production and AISC data from Mining Magazine is dated April 2026 but covers full-year 2025. This is acceptable but may not reflect 2026 guidance updates from competitors.

6. **Valuation metrics:** Trailing P/E and market cap data are drawn from Yahoo Finance, MarketWatch, and secondary sources (dated late 2025 to May 2026). These are not official company disclosures and should be treated as indicative. Analytical impact: valuation assumption analysis is directional rather than precise.

7. **Colombia sanctioning process outcome:** The February 2026 notification of a sanctioning process is a developing situation with no financial quantification available. Analytical impact: contingent liability cannot be estimated.

# News & Narrative Deep Research: AngloGold Ashanti PLC (AU)

Research conducted: 2026-05-11

## 1. Most Significant News in the Window

**Q1 2026 Earnings: Record Cash Generation Despite Revenue Miss**
AngloGold Ashanti reported Q1 2026 results on May 8, 2026, delivering record free cash flow of $1.2 billion, up 190% year-on-year, and headline earnings of $1.3 billion (252 US cents per share). EBITDA rose 130% YoY to $2.3 billion. Non-GAAP EPS of $2.52 beat the $2.21 consensus, but revenue of approximately $3.15 billion fell short of the ~$3.26 billion expected. The earnings release emphasized the translation of high gold prices into margins, with the average realized price reaching $4,863/oz, up 69% YoY. [Source: Business Wire / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration" / Date: 2026-05-08] *** [Source: Quiver Quant / Title: "ANGLOGOLD ASHANTI ($AU) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings" / Date: 2026-05-08] *

Gold production for the Group rose 1% YoY to 724,000 oz, while managed operations produced 666,000 oz. All-in sustaining costs (AISC) increased 19% YoY to $1,955/oz, reflecting higher royalty payments linked to the record gold price, underlying inflation in labor and contractor costs, and a 29% increase in sustaining capital. Total capital expenditure rose 39% to $467 million, including $162 million in non-sustaining capex directed primarily at Nevada growth projects. [Source: AFP.com / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release" / Date: 2026-03-31] *** [Source: Las Vegas Sun / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release" / Date: 2026-05-08] *

**Capital Return Bonanza: Record Dividend and $2 Billion Buyback**
On May 7, 2026, the Board approved a proposed share repurchase program of up to $2.0 billion, subject to shareholder approval. This followed the declaration of a record interim dividend of $585 million (116 US cents per share), compared to just 12.5 US cents per share in Q1 2025. The base dividend was topped up to 50% of free cash flow under the Company's policy. [Source: Business Wire / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration" / Date: 2026-05-08] *** [Source: Globe and Mail / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Files Q1 2026 Earnings Results Presentation with U.S. SEC" / Date: 2026-05-09] **

**Ghana Regulatory Pressure Intensifies**
Two separate regulatory developments in Ghana have emerged as material narrative drivers. First, Ghana's Minerals and Mining (Royalty) Regulations, 2025 (L.I. 2500/2501) came into force on March 10, 2026, introducing a sliding-scale royalty framework on gold from 5% up to 12% in response to international market prices. This increases the royalty rate at AGA Iduapriem from 5% to approximately 12% while gold remains above $4,500/oz; the Obuasi Tax Concession Agreement protects that asset with a 3%-5% sliding scale. [Source: AngloGold Ashanti Form 20-F / Title: "Report Suite 2025 - Form 20-F" / Date: 2026-03-26] ***

Second, on April 22, 2026, Reuters reported that Ghana's mining regulator directed AngloGold Ashanti, Newmont, and Zijin to shift mining operations to local contractors by December 2026 or face sanctions. AngloGold stated it already operates contract mining at Iduapriem through a 50/50 joint venture and has begun transitioning fully to a local contractor by year-end. [Source: Reuters / Title: "Ghana directs Newmont, AngloGold, Zijin to shift mining ops to local firms by December, sources say" / Date: 2026-04-22] ** [Source: Mining.com / Title: "Ghana directs Newmont, AngloGold, Zijin to shift mining ops to local firms by December" / Date: 2026-04-22] **

**Nevada Arthur Project PFS Establishes 4.9Moz Reserve**
In late March 2026, AngloGold published a Technical Report Summary and pre-feasibility study for the Arthur Gold Project in Nevada's Beatty Mining District, declaring a first-time Probable Mineral Reserve of 4.9 million ounces of gold and 7.8 million ounces of silver. The PFS supports an initial nine-year mine life averaging ~500,000 oz/year at an estimated AISC of $954/oz, with project capital of approximately $3.6 billion. A feasibility study is expected to be presented to the Board in June 2026 for approval. [Source: Globe and Mail / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Unveils 4.9Moz Reserve and PFS for Arthur Gold Project in Nevada" / Date: 2026-03-27] * [Source: AFP.com / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Builds on Award-Winning Discovery, Advancing Arthur Gold Project in Nevada" / Date: 2026-03-26] **

On April 24, 2026, following the close of Q1, a contractor was fatally injured at the Obuasi mine in Ghana following a release of waste material from an underground ore pass. The company launched a comprehensive investigation. [Source: Business Wire / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release and Dividend Declaration" / Date: 2026-05-08] *** [Source: Discovery Alert / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Achieves Record Free Cash Flow in Q1 2026" / Date: 2026-05-08] *

Management maintained 2026 production guidance and highlighted progress at Obuasi, Sukari, and the Nevada projects.

- **Oaktree Capital Management / Howard Marks** has held AngloGold Ashanti in its 13F portfolio since Q3 2023 and continues to maintain a position. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti plc (AU): Billionaire Howard Marks Continues to..." / Date: 2026-04-27] *
- **Broader institutional picture:** 533 institutional owners filed 13F/13D/G forms per Fintel data, though directional Q1 2026 fund flows were not yet available as of the research date. [Source: Fintel / Title: "AU - Stock Price, Institutional Ownership, Shareholders (NYSE)" / Date: 2026-05-10] *

Most recent as of: May 10, 2026.

### Retail & Social Sentiment
Retail and social media sentiment is not a material driver of the narrative. AngloGold Ashanti is not a meme-stock or retail-crowded name. Coverage on Reddit, StockTwits, and Twitter is minimal relative to institutional discourse. No evidence that retail positioning has affected price action. Marked explicitly as **lower-signal**.

### Short Interest
Short interest declined meaningfully through the quarter. As of April 15, 2026, short interest stood at 3.69 million shares, representing approximately 0.73% of the public float—a very low level. This marked a 51.8% decline from the prior month, with a short-interest ratio of roughly 0.9 days to cover. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti PLC (NYSE:AU) Short Interest Down 51.8% in March" / Date: 2026-04-12] * [Source: MarketWatch / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti PLC Stock Quote (U.S.: NYSE)" / Date: 2026-05-10] **

Most recent as of: April 15, 2026.

### Valuation Narrative
The market is debating whether AngloGold's premium valuation is justified by its cash flow generation or vulnerable to multiple compression. Bullish arguments center on a 6%+ FCF yield, a 3.6%+ dividend yield, and leverage to gold prices. Bearish or cautious arguments highlight a "rich P/E" (trading around 20x), the $3.6 billion Nevada capex overhang, and AISC inflation. The Simply Wall St model dispersion—fair value estimates ranging from $75 to $155—encapsulates the debate. [Source: Simply Wall St / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti (NYSE:AU) - Stock Analysis" / Date: 2026-05-07] * [Source: Seeking Alpha / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti: Surging Free Cash Flow As Gold Consolidates" / Date: 2026-05-08] *

---

## 7. Short-Seller Research & Activist Investor Involvement

No short-seller reports or activist investor activity were identified in the research window. Short interest is below 1% of float, suggesting the market does not currently view the stock as a compelling short candidate. No 13D activist filings or public demands were found.

---

## 8. Narrative Shifts

A discernible narrative shift has occurred over the window.

**Prior Narrative (Late 2025–Early 2026):** AngloGold was primarily framed as a turnaround and gold-leverage story—detaching from the South African discount, successfully integrating Centamin/Sukari, ramping Obuasi, and riding the gold price surge toward $5,600/oz. The focus was on production growth and portfolio upgrade.

**Current Narrative (May 2026):** The story has pivoted to a "cash-flow and capital-return powerhouse facing cost and jurisdictional headwinds." The Q1 earnings reinforced this: record FCF and dividends dominated headlines, but AISC inflation (+19%), Ghana's royalty and local-content squeeze, and the April contractor fatality at Obuasi introduced operational and regulatory questions. Meanwhile, gold's pullback from record highs to the ~$4,700/oz range has made the market more sensitive to cost structures.

**Trigger:** The confluence of the March 10 Ghana royalty implementation, the April 22 local-contractor directive, and the Q1 cost metrics released May 8 shifted the conversation from "how high can gold take this" to "how much of the gold price can the company keep after costs and government take."

---

## 9. Narrative-vs-Reality Disconnects

**Disconnect 1: Capital Return Enthusiasm vs. Rising Costs and Regulatory Pressure**
The dominant bullish narrative emphasizes record free cash flow, a $2 billion buyback, and a 116cps dividend. However, AISC rose 19% YoY to $1,955/oz, Ghana is extracting a larger share via royalties and local-content mandates, and the Nevada project will require $3.6 billion in capex. The market appears to be pricing continued gold prices well above $4,500/oz while dismissing the structural cost and fiscal pressures that rise in tandem with the gold price.

**Disconnect 2: Insider Selling vs. Board Confidence**
The Board approved a record $2 billion buyback and massive dividend increase, yet CEO Alberto Calderon sold approximately $13 million of stock (22% of his stake) in April 2026, and no insiders purchased shares in the trailing year. The buyback narrative reads as confidence; the insider flow reads as diversification or valuation sensitivity.

**Disconnect 3: Low Short Interest vs. Visible Risks**
Short interest sits below 1% of float, implying broad market complacency. Yet Ghanaian regulatory risk is intensifying, AISC is trending up, and the Nevada project carries significant execution risk. The absence of short-seller skepticism may leave the stock vulnerable to a narrative correction if costs or regulatory headwinds worsen.

---

## 10. Bottom-Line Narrative Assessment

### Narrative Characterization
AngloGold Ashanti is currently viewed as a best-in-class cash generator among large-cap gold miners, capitalizing on elevated gold prices to return record capital to shareholders while advancing a Tier One Nevada growth project. However, the gloss is cracking slightly under the weight of Ghanaian regulatory extraction, cost inflation, and a post-fatality safety reminder. The stock is no longer just a gold-beta play; it is being scrutinized as a complex global operator with a growing government-take problem.

### Key Narrative Drivers (confidence weighted)
1. **Gold price leverage and record cash generation** — *High confidence*. Q1 FCF of $1.2B and the 69% higher realized price are hard data points. The dividend and buyback confirm management's willingness to distribute. [Source: Business Wire / Title: "AngloGold Ashanti Q1 31 March 2026 Earnings Release" / Date: 2026-05-08] ***
2. **Ghana regulatory and fiscal pressure** — *High confidence*. Two concrete regulations (royalty sliding scale effective March 10; local contractor mandate with December 2026 deadline) are confirmed by primary sources and Reuters. [Source: AngloGold Ashanti Form 20-F / Date: 2026-03-26] *** [Source: Reuters / Date: 2026-04-22] **
3. **Nevada Arthur Project as long-term growth anchor** — *Medium confidence*. The 4.9Moz reserve and PFS are real, but the $3.6B capex and June 2026 Board approval gate mean the project is still pre-commitment. Permitting and community risk remain manageable but not zero. [Source: Globe and Mail / Date: 2026-03-27] *
4. **Cost inflation and AISC trajectory** — *Medium confidence*. Q1 AISC of $1,955/oz (+19% YoY) is confirmed, but the market is debating whether this is a mechanical royalty effect or structural inflation. Management's "control what we can control" language suggests limited ability to bend the cost curve if royalties and labor inflation persist. [Source: AFP.com / Date: 2026-03-31] ***
5. **Obuasi ramp-up and safety performance** — *Medium confidence*. Production improved (+8k oz YoY), but the April 24 fatality and the complexity of underground ramp-ups remain risks. [Source: Business Wire / Date: 2026-05-08] ***

### Narrative Risks (confidence weighted)
1. **Further Ghanaian fiscal extraction or contract renegotiation** — *High risk / Medium confidence*. The Obuasi Tax Concession Agreement expires December 31, 2027. If Ghana seeks to renegotiate stability terms before or at renewal, the market's bullish cash-flow assumptions for the country's ~15% of production could fracture. [Source: AngloGold Ashanti Form 20-F / Date: 2026-03-26] ***
2. **Gold price correction below $4,500/oz** — *High risk / High confidence*. At $1,955/oz AISC and rising, margins compress quickly if gold retreats. The company's own planning assumption of ~$4,250/oz implies thinner margins than the current spot environment. [Source: AFP.com / Date: 2026-03-31] ***
3. **Nevada capex execution and permitting delays** — *Medium risk / Medium confidence*. A $3.6 billion project in a water-scarce region with NEPA requirements is inherently exposed to cost overruns and timeline slippage.
4. **Insider selling eroding confidence** — *Low risk / Medium confidence*. The CEO's $13M sale is not massive in the context of a $50B market cap, but it contrasts awkwardly with the buyback/dividend signaling.
5. **Safety and operational disruptions at Obuasi** — *Medium risk / Low confidence*. One fatality does not make a trend, but underground ore-pass incidents can trigger regulatory scrutiny and production pauses.

### Catalysts to Watch
- **June 2026:** Arthur Gold Project feasibility study presented to Board for approval. [Source: Globe and Mail / Date: 2026-03-27] *
- **August 2026 (expected):** Q2 2026 earnings release. Key focus: AISC trajectory, Sukari production, and any Ghana cost guidance updates.
- **December 2026:** Deadline for Ghana local-contractor transition. [Source: Reuters / Date: 2026-04-22] **
- **May 29, 2026:** Ex-dividend date for interim dividend; payment June 12, 2026. [Source: Business Wire / Date: 2026-05-08] ***
- **Late 2026 / 2027:** Potential renewal negotiations for Obuasi Tax Concession Agreement (expires December 31, 2027). [Source: AngloGold Ashanti Form 20-F / Date: 2026-03-26] ***

---

## 11. Data Gaps & Limitations

1. **Earnings Call Q&A Transcript:** No verified transcript of the analyst Q&A session from the May 8, 2026 call was accessible. Morningstar lists the transcript but the content was not retrievable. This limits the ability to characterize pushback, tone shifts, or management evasion during questioning. *Search scope: Morningstar, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat, Quartr.* [Source: Morningstar / Title: "ANG - Anglogold Ashanti PLC Earnings Call Transcripts" / Date: 2026-05-08] **

2. **Q1 2026 13F Institutional Flows:** Detailed Q1 2026 13F filings (covering the period ended March 31, 2026) were not yet available as of the research date; only Q4 2025 data was accessible. This leaves a gap in real-time institutional sentiment. *Search scope: Fintel, MarketBeat, SECForm4, HedgeFollow.*

3. **2026 AISC Guidance Range:** The company provided production guidance (2.80–3.17 Moz) and cost assumptions for planning purposes, but a formal, specific AISC guidance range for full-year 2026 was not located in available sources. *Search scope: Zacks, MarketBeat, company press release.*

4. **Analyst Rating Changes:** No verifiable, dated sell-side upgrades or downgrades from named firms (other than RBC's maintained Buy) were identified within the 90-day window. The consensus data is drawn from aggregate sources rather than discrete analyst actions. *Search scope: Barchart, Benzinga, MarketScreener, TipRanks.*

5. **Short Interest Discrepancy:** Sources reported conflicting short-interest trends (a 51.8% decline in March per MarketBeat versus a slight uptick to 3.69M shares by mid-April per MarketWatch). The absolute level is low in either case, but the directional trend is ambiguous. *Search scope: MarketBeat, MarketWatch, Fintel, Benzinga.*
